question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the absence of rate limiting on `getHookStorage()` to perform a brute-force enumeration of all storage keys for any hook ID and owner ID combination, systematically exfiltrating all hook storage data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `getKeysInRange()` method's stream-based filtering to cause excessive CPU consumption by supplying a very large `keys` collection with entries that all pass the range filter, causing the `hookStorageRepository` to execute a massive `IN` query?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged user set `keys` to a collection containing a single empty byte array `[]` in `HookStorageRequest` to probe whether a zero-length key exists in a hook's storage, leaking the existence of a sentinel or default storage entry?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that `getHookStorageChange()` does not filter by `deleted=false` (unlike the non-historical path which uses `DeletedIsFalse`) to read storage entries that were deleted, leaking data that should be hidden?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged user supply a `hookIds` collection with `Long.MAX_VALUE` entries in `HooksRequest` to cause the `idsInRange` stream filter to consume excessive CPU and memory, causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHooks(HooksRequest)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the `idsInRange` filter in `getHooks()` (which uses `hookId >= lowerBound && hookId <= upperBound`) with `lowerBound=Long.MIN_VALUE` and `upperBound=Long.MAX_VALUE` to ensure all submitted hook IDs pass the filter, then pass an unbounded list to `findByOwnerIdAndHookIdIn`?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/service/HookService.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getHookStorage(HookStorageRequest)] Can an unprivileged user set `hookId` to `Long.MAX_VALUE` in `HookStorageRequest` to probe whether any hook with the maximum possible ID exists, leaking information about the hook ID space boundaries?
